questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop completely and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a full stop behind the stop line and wait until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady yellow traffic light?
choices: Stop if you can do so safely, because the light is about to turn red
Always speed up to beat the red light
Treat it the same as a green light
Come to a complete stop and wait for green
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so stop if it is safe rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, the same as a stop sign
Keep moving without stopping
The signal is broken, so ignore it
Yield only to vehicles on your left
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After stopping completely, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
At any time without stopping
Only when a green arrow is showing
Never, right turns on red are not allowed
questions.explanation: Arkansas allows a right turn on red after a complete stop, provided no sign forbids it and traffic and pedestrians are clear.

questions.prompt: What does an eight-sided (octagon) sign always mean?
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
No passing
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs so drivers recognize it even from behind.

questions.prompt: What color and shape are most warning signs that alert you to hazards ahead?
choices: Yellow diamond
Red octagon
Green rectangle
Blue square
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn of hazards such as curves, intersections, or pedestrian crossings ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop and wait for a green light
Stop completely, then proceed
Lane is closed ahead
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the road mean?
choices: Do not pass
Passing is allowed when clear
Two-way left turn lane
Edge of the roadway
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe there.

questions.prompt: What does an orange construction sign indicate?
choices: A work zone or construction area ahead
A school crossing
A scenic route
A required exit
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and maintenance zones where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power outage, you should treat the intersection as:
choices: A four-way stop
A green light for all directions
A yield for cross traffic only
An intersection where the larger road has priority
questions.explanation: When signals are dark, every driver should stop and proceed in turn as if it were an all-way stop.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians crossing your path
Only vehicles behind you
No one, because you are turning
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked crosswalk?
choices: Pedestrians
Drivers turning right
Drivers going straight
Whoever arrives first
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, who has the right-of-way?
choices: Vehicles already in the roundabout
Vehicles entering the roundabout
The vehicle moving fastest
The vehicle in the outside lane
questions.explanation: You must yield to traffic already circulating in the roundabout before you enter.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until the emergency vehicle passes.

questions.prompt: When entering a highway from a driveway or alley, you must:
choices: Yield to all traffic and pedestrians on the road you are entering
Enter quickly before traffic arrives
Assume other drivers will slow for you
Stop only if a sign requires it
questions.explanation: Traffic already on the through road has the right-of-way, so you must yield when entering from a driveway or alley.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal in Arkansas?
choices: At least 100 feet before the turn
Only at the moment you turn
About 10 feet before the turn
Signaling is optional
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to anticipate your turn.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to vehicles that arrived first and to the vehicle on your right
Always go first
Speed through quickly
Stop and wait for a signal to appear
questions.explanation: At uncontrolled intersections, yield to vehicles already there and to the driver on your right when arriving together.

questions.prompt: Before making a turn, where should you position your vehicle?
choices: In the correct lane well ahead of the turn
In any lane until the last second
In the center of the road
Always in the far right lane
questions.explanation: Moving into the proper turn lane early lets other drivers predict your path and reduces conflicts.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn but a pedestrian is crossing the road you are entering, you should:
choices: Wait and let the pedestrian finish crossing
Turn around them quickly
Honk so they hurry
Proceed because vehicles have priority
questions.explanation: Turning drivers must yield to pedestrians legally crossing the street they are entering.

questions.prompt: What is the typical default speed limit in an Arkansas urban district unless otherwise posted?
choices: 30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
20 mph
questions.explanation: In Arkansas urban districts the default speed limit is generally 30 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

questions.prompt: Many rural interstate highways in Arkansas are posted at a maximum speed of:
choices: 70 mph
55 mph
80 mph
60 mph
questions.explanation: Rural interstates in Arkansas are commonly posted at 70 mph, with up to 75 mph on certain designated rural freeways.

questions.prompt: What does the basic speed law require, regardless of the posted limit?
choices: Driving at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for current conditions
Always driving exactly the posted limit
Driving as fast as traffic allows
Driving 5 mph below the limit at all times
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires a safe speed for conditions, which may be below the posted limit in rain, fog, or traffic.

questions.prompt: On a dry road, what following distance does the three-second rule provide?
choices: A safe gap so you can react and stop if the vehicle ahead slows
Exactly three car lengths at any speed
A gap only needed on highways
The minimum legal speed
questions.explanation: The three-second rule keeps enough space ahead to react and brake safely on dry pavement.

questions.prompt: In poor conditions such as rain or fog, you should adjust your following distance by:
choices: Increasing it to four or more seconds
Keeping it at three seconds
Decreasing it to stay close
Removing all following distance
questions.explanation: Wet or low-visibility conditions lengthen stopping distance, so increase your following gap to four seconds or more.

questions.prompt: When driving through a school zone with children present, you should:
choices: Slow to the posted reduced limit and watch for children
Maintain the normal road speed
Sound your horn to clear the way
Speed up to pass through quickly
questions.explanation: School zones have reduced limits and increased fines, so slow down and stay alert for children.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Blocks traffic flow and can cause crashes
Always saves fuel safely
Is required in all lanes
Improves your reaction time
questions.explanation: Going too slowly impedes traffic and can lead to crashes; keep pace with the safe flow of traffic.

questions.prompt: A 'space cushion' around your vehicle helps you:
choices: Have room to brake, steer, or stop to avoid a collision
Drive faster than other vehicles
Ignore your mirrors
Tailgate more safely
questions.explanation: Keeping space ahead, behind, and to the sides gives you an escape path to avoid crashes.

questions.prompt: When approaching a curve, you should:
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Accelerate sharply into the curve
Maintain highway speed throughout
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps you keep control, since braking hard mid-curve can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: Stopping distance increases when:
choices: Your speed increases or the road is wet
You lower your speed
Tires are new and roads are dry
You increase following distance
questions.explanation: Higher speeds and slippery roads both lengthen the distance needed to stop.

questions.prompt: You should never pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
The road is straight and clear
Traffic is light
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side marks a no-passing zone where passing is unsafe and illegal.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right and use left lanes only to pass
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes constantly
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should keep right, leaving the left lanes open for passing.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Your front bumper clears theirs
You begin the pass
The other driver flashes their lights
questions.explanation: Returning only after seeing the vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room to merge back safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check mirrors and your blind spot
Only check your mirror
Change quickly without signaling
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors plus your blind spot over your shoulder prevents collisions when changing lanes.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to pass another vehicle:
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
On a long straight road
On a divided highway with passing lanes
When the road is dry
questions.explanation: Passing where your view is blocked, such as hills and curves, is illegal because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly and stay in your lane
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move toward the center line
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Holding your speed or easing off lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: To complete a pass, is it legal to exceed the posted speed limit?
choices: No, you may not exceed the speed limit even to pass
Yes, by up to 10 mph
Yes, on any highway
Only at night
questions.explanation: Speed limits apply even while passing, so do not exceed the limit to complete a pass.

questions.prompt: Passing on the shoulder of the road is:
choices: Not allowed
Allowed in heavy traffic
Allowed at any time
Allowed only on the right
questions.explanation: The shoulder is not a travel lane, so passing there is prohibited and dangerous.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
Passing is never allowed
The lane is for turning only
You must change lanes
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line allows passing from your side when oncoming traffic is clear.

questions.prompt: You should avoid passing within an intersection because:
choices: Cross traffic and turning vehicles increase the risk of a crash
It is always faster to wait
Intersections have no markings
Passing there is encouraged
questions.explanation: Intersections bring vehicles from other directions, making passing there especially hazardous.

questions.prompt: Under Arkansas's Move Over law, when you approach a stopped vehicle with flashing lights, you must:
choices: Change lanes away from it or slow to a safe, cautious speed
Maintain your speed and stay in your lane
Speed up to pass it quickly
Stop in the road
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires drivers to move over a lane or slow down for stopped emergency, utility, and highway vehicles displaying flashing lights.

questions.prompt: When you see a school bus stopped with red lights flashing and its stop arm out, you must:
choices: Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing and the arm folds in
Pass slowly on the left
Honk and proceed
Slow to 20 mph and continue
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights and an extended stop arm mean children are loading or unloading, so you must stop.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering beside them and never cut in closely in front
Drive in their blind spots
Tailgate to save space
Pass on the right only
questions.explanation: Trucks cannot see vehicles in their blind spots and need long stopping distances, so stay visible and leave room.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with motorcycles, you should:
choices: Look twice and allow them a full lane
Share the lane beside them
Follow more closely than with cars
Pass without signaling
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easy to miss, so check carefully and give them a full lane width.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Slow down and give them plenty of room
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to move them aside
Expect them to ride on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are legitimate road users, so pass them slowly and with a safe amount of space.

questions.prompt: A large truck turning right may:
choices: Swing wide to the left before completing the turn
Always stay tight to the curb
Turn faster than a car
Never need extra space
questions.explanation: Trucks often swing wide to make right turns, so never try to squeeze between the truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is crossing at an intersection without signals, you should:
choices: Yield and allow them to cross safely
Proceed because vehicles have the right-of-way
Honk and continue
Drive around them quickly
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at intersections, whether or not the crosswalk is marked.

questions.prompt: Why do large trucks need more distance to stop than passenger cars?
choices: They are much heavier and take longer to slow down
Their brakes are weaker by law
They travel faster than cars
They have smaller tires
questions.explanation: A loaded truck's weight greatly increases stopping distance, so never cut in front and brake suddenly.

questions.prompt: When you encounter slow-moving farm equipment on a rural road, you should:
choices: Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Pass immediately on a curve
Tailgate until it pulls over
Honk continuously
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so reduce speed and pass only where you have a clear, legal view ahead.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, a driver age 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of:
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.02 percent or higher
questions.explanation: Arkansas's per se limit for drivers 21 and older is a BAC of 0.08 percent.

questions.prompt: Under Arkansas's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be charged for driving with a BAC as low as:
choices: 0.02 percent
0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: For drivers under 21, Arkansas uses a zero-tolerance limit of 0.02 percent BAC.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Arkansas is:
choices: Banned for all drivers
Allowed for drivers over 21
Allowed at red lights
Legal on highways
questions.explanation: Arkansas bans texting while driving for every driver regardless of age.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, drivers under 18 may use a cell phone while driving:
choices: Not at all, even hands-free
Only for texting
Only on the highway
Whenever they want
questions.explanation: Arkansas prohibits drivers under 18 from using a cell phone while driving, including in hands-free mode.

questions.prompt: What is the only effective way to lower your blood alcohol concentration?
choices: Time
Drinking coffee
Eating a large meal
Taking a cold shower
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to remove alcohol; coffee, food, and showers do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what else can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medications
Wearing a seat belt
Using your turn signals
Driving below the speed limit
questions.explanation: Legal medicines can cause drowsiness or impairment, and driving while impaired by them can lead to a DWI.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of distracted driving?
choices: Reading or sending a text message
Scanning the road ahead
Checking your mirrors
Keeping both hands on the wheel
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes, hands, and mind off driving, making it a dangerous distraction.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, all drivers must use hands-free technology when using a phone in:
choices: Active school zones and work zones
Residential neighborhoods
Parking lots
Rural highways
questions.explanation: Arkansas requires hands-free phone use in active school zones and work zones where workers or children are present.

questions.prompt: If you are too impaired to drive safely, the best choice is to:
choices: Arrange another way home, such as a sober driver or rideshare
Drive slowly and carefully
Take back roads home
Roll down the windows and drive
questions.explanation: Never drive while impaired; use a sober driver, rideshare, or other transportation instead.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Improving your reflexes
Sharpening your vision
Making you a safer driver
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions and impairs judgment and coordination, greatly increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, when must you turn on your headlights?
choices: Whenever visibility is poor and any time your windshield wipers are in use
Only after midnight
Only on the interstate
Only when it is completely dark
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires headlights when visibility is poor and whenever your wipers are operating.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer straight
Brake hard immediately
Accelerate to regain traction
Turn the wheel sharply
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering straight lets the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams give you the best view.

questions.prompt: Why is the road especially slippery during the first rain after a dry spell?
choices: Oil and grime rise to the surface and mix with water
The rain freezes instantly
Tires grip better when wet
The road is always cleaner
questions.explanation: Built-up oil rises with the first rain, creating a slick surface until it washes away.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road typically freezes first in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Hills
City streets
Gravel roads
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from above and below, so they freeze before other road surfaces.

questions.prompt: Driving at night, you should not 'overdrive your headlights,' which means:
choices: You should be able to stop within the distance you can see ahead
You should use only your high beams
You should drive faster to see better
You should keep your interior lights on
questions.explanation: Overdriving your headlights means going too fast to stop within the lit distance, which is unsafe at night.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, you should:
choices: Steer gently in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Slam on the brakes
Steer sharply away from the skid
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Steering smoothly in your intended direction helps you regain control during a skid.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog you should adjust your driving by:
choices: Slowing down and increasing your following distance
Speeding up to get through faster
Following closely to see better
Using your high beams
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility mean you should slow down and leave more space ahead.

questions.prompt: If you are driving and your view is suddenly blocked by heavy fog, the safest action is to:
choices: Slow down and, if needed, pull completely off the road and turn on hazards
Speed up to get out of the fog
Stop in your travel lane
Turn off your lights
questions.explanation: In dense fog, reduce speed and, if you cannot see, pull fully off the road and use your hazard lights.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas you may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 15 feet
5 feet
25 feet
50 feet
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so emergency crews can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill stops the car from rolling into traffic if it slips.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Either direction
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away so the tires catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street, you should:
choices: Check for passing vehicles and bicyclists
Open it quickly to save time
Assume the road is clear
Honk first
questions.explanation: Checking for traffic and cyclists before opening your door prevents 'dooring' crashes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an illegal place to park?
choices: In a crosswalk
In a marked parking space
In a driveway you own with permission
In a public parking lot
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When you leave your parked vehicle on a hill, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
Leave it in neutral
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors unlocked
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving it in park or gear keeps the vehicle from rolling.

questions.prompt: When pulling away from a curb into traffic, you should:
choices: Signal and yield to traffic already on the road
Pull out immediately
Expect traffic to stop for you
Back up first
questions.explanation: Signal your intent and yield to passing traffic before merging from a parked position.

questions.prompt: You should never stop or park where:
choices: You block traffic or a clear view of the road
A parking space is marked
There is a wide shoulder designated for parking
A sign permits parking
questions.explanation: Stopping where you block traffic or sight lines creates a hazard and is not allowed.

questions.prompt: A curb painted to indicate a no-parking zone or a posted no-parking sign means you should:
choices: Not park there
Park only briefly
Park if you turn on hazards
Park overnight only
questions.explanation: Posted signs and painted curbs that restrict parking must be obeyed.

questions.prompt: At what age can you obtain an Arkansas learner's license?
choices: 14 to 15 years old
16 years old
18 years old
13 years old
questions.explanation: Arkansas allows a learner's license at ages 14 to 15 as the first stage of graduated licensing.

questions.prompt: While holding an Arkansas learner's license, you may drive only when accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old
Any licensed driver
A driver at least 18 years old
Another permit holder
questions.explanation: A learner's license holder must be supervised by a licensed driver who is at least 21.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Arkansas written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Arkansas written knowledge test consists of 25 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: What score is required to pass the Arkansas written knowledge test?
choices: 80 percent
70 percent
90 percent
60 percent
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass the Arkansas knowledge test.

questions.prompt: An Arkansas intermediate license is generally available to drivers who are:
choices: 16 to 17 years old
14 years old
18 to 20 years old
21 or older
questions.explanation: The intermediate license stage applies to Arkansas drivers ages 16 to 17.

questions.prompt: Under Arkansas's intermediate license rules, you may not drive between:
choices: 11:00 p.m. and 4:00 a.m. unless an exception applies
9:00 p.m. and 5:00 a.m. with no exceptions
Midnight and 6:00 a.m.
questions.explanation: Intermediate drivers cannot drive from 11 p.m. to 4 a.m. unless with an adult 21 or older or going to or from school, church, work, or an emergency.

questions.prompt: An Arkansas intermediate license holder may carry how many unrelated minor passengers without an adult 21 or older present?
choices: No more than one
No more than two
As many as fit in the vehicle
Three
questions.explanation: Intermediate drivers may have only one unrelated minor passenger unless a licensed adult 21 or older is in the vehicle.

questions.prompt: Applicants under 18 applying for an Arkansas license must also provide:
choices: Proof of school enrollment and parental consent
A high school diploma
Proof of employment
A passport
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 must show proof of school enrollment and have a parent or guardian sign consent.

questions.prompt: Besides the written test, what else must you pass to be licensed in Arkansas?
choices: A vision screening
A swimming test
A first-aid exam
A typing test
questions.explanation: Applicants must pass a vision screening in addition to the knowledge and driving tests.

questions.prompt: Under Arkansas's implied-consent law, what happens if you refuse a lawful chemical test after a DWI arrest?
choices: Your driver's license is automatically suspended
Nothing happens
You receive only a warning
You can retake the test later
questions.explanation: By driving in Arkansas you consent to chemical testing, and refusing one results in an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: In Arkansas, all front-seat occupants of a vehicle must:
choices: Wear a seat belt
Sit on the right side
Be over 18
Hold a license
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires all front-seat occupants to wear seat belts.

questions.prompt: A conviction for DWI in Arkansas can result in:
choices: Fines, possible jail time, and license suspension
Only a written warning
A small parking ticket
No consequences for a first offense
questions.explanation: A DWI conviction carries fines, possible jail, and license suspension, with harsher penalties for repeat or high-BAC offenses.

questions.prompt: What documents should you always carry while driving in Arkansas?
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
Only your license
A birth certificate
Your vehicle title
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and show their license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance.

questions.prompt: Young children who are too small for a seat belt must be secured in:
choices: An appropriate child restraint or booster seat
The front seat with a lap belt
A parent's lap
The cargo area
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires young children to be secured in a proper child restraint or booster seat based on age and size.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you are required to:
choices: Stop and remain at the scene to exchange information
Leave the scene if no police are present
Drive away if your car still works
Wait only if someone is injured
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is illegal; you must stop, help if needed, and exchange information.

questions.prompt: Violating Arkansas's Move Over law can result in:
choices: A fine and other penalties
Only a verbal warning
No penalty at all
A reward
questions.explanation: Failing to move over or slow down for stopped emergency and service vehicles is an enforceable offense with penalties.

questions.prompt: Repeated traffic violations can lead to:
choices: License suspension or revocation
Automatic license renewal
A higher speed limit
No consequences
questions.explanation: Accumulating violations can result in suspension or revocation of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Driving without valid liability insurance in Arkansas is:
choices: Illegal and subject to penalties
Allowed for new drivers
Legal on local roads
Optional
questions.explanation: Arkansas requires liability insurance, and driving without it carries fines and other penalties.
